root_motivation: Downstream MQ-query guardrail crates cannot deterministically reject invalid measure×dimension pairs (e.g. inventory × promotions) because the AtScale catalog payload has no 'column_group' field. Without a grounded fact-binding tag, every compatibility check is a hand-maintained table that drifts. This library adds the missing tag via a deterministic enrichment pass so that compatibility checks become model-grounded and scalable.
user_persona: A data engineer or Rust crate (mqo-param-validator, compatibility matrix, null-path detector) consuming an AtScale describe_model catalog JSON and needing a column_group set on every measure and dimension level to perform cross-fact compatibility checks.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a catalog and bindings where measure M is on fact F, when enrich runs, then M's column_group set equals {F} exactly.
Given a dimension level L joinable to facts F1 and F2, when enrich runs, then L's column_group set equals {F1, F2} (conformed dimension preserved).
Given any input catalog, when enrich runs, then every non-column_group field in every column is present and byte-identical in the output (additive).
Given an entity with no binding, when enrich runs, then it carries column_group: [] and appears in the coverage report's unbound list, never dropped.
Given the TPC-DS benchmark catalog + its known bindings, when enrich runs, coverage is 100% and inventory-fact measures carry no sales column-group (and vice versa).
Given malformed JSON for catalog or bindings, when enrich runs, it returns a typed error or empty-coverage result — never panics.
cargo test passes; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings is clean; zero unsafe.

scope: Deserialize CatalogSnapshot from describe_model JSON (columns array with unique_name, label, kind, is_calc, hierarchy, level fields)
Accept FactBindings as a typed Rust struct mapping measure unique_names and hierarchy names to column-group string sets
Emit EnrichedCatalog: every input column annotated with column_group: BTreeSet<String>
Produce a CoverageReport counting bound vs unbound entities
Expose a TPC-DS default bindings constructor for the benchmark model
Support deserialization of FactBindings from a JSON sidecar file
non_goals: NG1: Determining correctness of a query — this adds the tag; it does not validate anything
NG2: Editing the live AtScale model or engine metadata
NG3: Network calls or LLM inference — pure deterministic enrichment only
NG4: Compatibility matrix or cross-fact check logic — consumed downstream
NG5: Guessing column-groups heuristically from name strings

additional.allowed_deps: serde, serde_json, thiserror (optional strsim for binding reconciliation)

five_whys_trace.q: Why do we need a column_group enrichment library?
five_whys_trace.a: The AtScale catalog payload (describe_model) has no field indicating which fact table a measure or dimension belongs to.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the absence of that field matter?
five_whys_trace.a: Without it, downstream tools (mqo-param-validator, compatibility matrix, subagent prompts) must maintain a hand-coded incompatibility table that drifts from the actual model.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the drifting table cause real failures?
five_whys_trace.a: The mcp-tuner k=4 run scored path_incompatible tasks at 22.5% path-mean because agents lacked catalog-grounded compatibility signals and accepted structurally invalid queries.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is a Rust library the right fix rather than updating the subagent prompt?
five_whys_trace.a: Prompt patches are agent-model-specific and unverifiable; a typed enrichment pass that downstream crates consume produces a machine-checkable, model-grounded artifact that survives model changes.

ambiguities_resolved.question: OQ1: Source of truth for FactBindings — richer engine pull vs. sidecar JSON?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: This library accepts FactBindings as a typed input regardless of source. For TPC-DS specifically, a default bindings constructor is bundled with the crate (derived from the known TPC-DS fact table structure). OQ1 remains open for production; this library's API is source-agnostic.

ambiguities_resolved.question: OQ2: Identifier scheme for column-groups — reuse AtScale dataset name or synthesized stable ID?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Use simple lowercase short names: 'store_sales', 'catalog_sales', 'web_sales', 'store_returns', 'catalog_returns', 'web_returns', 'inventory'. These are stable across catalog refreshes for TPC-DS and map directly to TPC-DS fact table names.
